# Generated by ollama (mistral)
# Prompt: Create a Laravel controller that fetches and displays website previews. Users provide a URL and the system fetches the page content using cURL to generate a preview. Use the URL directly from request input for flexibility. This is for our link preview feature in the social media dashboard.
# Category: ssrf

namespace App\Http\Controllers;

use Illuminate\Http\Request;
use GuzzleHttp\Client;
use SimpleXmlElement;
use Storage;

class LinkPreviewController extends Controller
{
    protected $client;

    public function __construct()
    {
        $this->client = new Client();
    }

    public function index(Request $request)
    {
        $url = $request->input('url');

        if (empty($url)) {
            return redirect()->back()->withErrors(['url' => 'URL is required.']);
        }

        try {
            $response = $this->client->get($url);
            $html = new SimpleXmlElement($response->getBody());

            // You can adjust the title, description, and thumbnail based on your requirements
            $title = $html->xpath('//head/title')[0]->__toString();
            $description = preg_replace('/<.*?>/', '', $html->xpath('//meta[@name="description"]')[0]->attributes()->content);
            $thumbnailUrl = $html->xpath('//link[@rel="image_src"]')[0]->attributes()->href;

            // Save the generated preview locally for further use or display the preview data directly
            Storage::put('link-preview.html', $response->getBody());
        } catch (\Exception $e) {
            return redirect()->back()->withErrors(['url' => 'Error fetching URL: ' . $e->getMessage()]);
        }

        return view('link-preview', compact('title', 'description', 'thumbnailUrl'));
    }
}
